title: Robot beats human half-marathon record after falling - TCR 04/21/26
description:
An Honor humanoid robot beat the human half-marathon world record by seven minutes in Beijing after falling near the finish line, Fervo Energy filed for IPO with 3.65 GW of enhanced geothermal, and Amazon invested $5 billion in Anthropic for gigawatts of Trainium compute.
This episode covers the Beijing robot marathon's 68% year-over-year improvement, circular compute economics binding frontier AI to cloud infrastructure, Fervo's geothermal cost trajectory, MISO's 35% load growth forecast, Siemens's Eigen Engineering Agent, vitamin B7 as a cancer metabolism vulnerability, and Deezer's disclosure that 44% of new music uploads are synthetic.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-21-2026/

0:00 Honor robot beats human half-marathon record in Beijing
0:36 50 Minutes, 26 Seconds
3:17 Fervo Energy IPO and enhanced geothermal cost trajectory
10:17 Deezer reports 44% of uploads AI-generated
15:32 What shipped
16:19 Close

Three hundred robots from roughly 100 Chinese institutions ran a public half-marathon alongside 12,000 human runners, with the fastest autonomous robot finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds - a 68% improvement over the same event twelve months ago. Amazon's $5 billion investment in Anthropic secures up to 5 GW of compute capacity through Trainium4 chips that have not yet been fabricated, binding Anthropic's research trajectory to Amazon's silicon roadmap for the rest of the decade in the third such circular deal in recent months. Fervo Energy's IPO filing disclosed 42 GW of leased geothermal potential and a 70% reduction in drilling costs since 2022, with engineers who learned horizontal drilling in shale fields now driving the buildout. MISO projects data centers will consume a quarter of all Midwestern electricity by 2040. Siemens shipped a generally available AI agent that writes PLC code and configures industrial devices in production across 19 countries. University of Lausanne researchers identified biotin deprivation as a way to block cancer cells' metabolic escape route through pyruvate carboxylase. And Deezer reported 75,000 AI-generated tracks uploaded per day with 97% listener indistinguishability - more synthetic music daily on one platform than the entire global recording industry released per day as recently as 2019.
